articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Sweden celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Linnea Rheborg - UEFA/UEFA via Getty Images

images.name: Scotland celebrate [1296x729]
images.credit: Alan Harvey/SNS Group via Getty Images

images.name: World Cup trophy New York [1296x729]
images.credit: Mike Stobe - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure
images.caption: Gab Marcotti reacts to Italy failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup after losing to Bosnia and Herzegovina on penalties.

articles.headline: Could Morocco beat Brazil in Group C at the World Cup?
articles.description: Sam Ogunleye and Ed Dove discuss the strength of Morocco's squad as well as the potential to defeat group favourites Brazil.

images.caption: Sam Ogunleye and Ed Dove discuss the strength of Morocco's squad as well as the potential to defeat group favourites Brazil.

articles.headline: New Adidas World Cup jerseys rated: Argentina, Mexico, Spain
articles.description: Adidas has released a huge batch of home kits for 22 countries, many of whom have either qualified or still hope to qualify for the FIFA 2026 World Cup.

article.headline: Ancelotti hails 'wonderful 15 days' as Brazil qualifies for 2026 World Cup finals
article.description: VIDEO SHOWS: SOUNDBITES FROM BRAZIL'S COACH CARLO ANCELOTTI AFTER THEY QUALIFIED FOR 2026 WORLD CUP FINALS
article.linkText: Ancelotti hails 'wonderful 15 days' as Brazil qualifies for 2026 World Cup finals

images.name: How Brazil clinched their spot for the 2026 World Cup
images.caption: Brazil qualified for the 2026 World Cup with a 1-0 win over Paraguay on Tuesday night in Sao Paulo.

article.story:
VIDEO SHOWS: SOUNDBITES FROM BRAZIL'S COACH CARLO ANCELOTTI AFTER THEY QUALIFIED FOR 2026 WORLD CUP FINALS RESENDING WITH COMPLETE SCRIPT AND SHOTLIST SHOWS: SAO PAULO, BRAZIL (JUNE 10, 2025) (REUTERS - Access all) 1. BRAZIL COACH CARLO ANCELOTTI ARRIVING FOR THE PRESS CONFERENCE 2. (SOUNDBITE) (Spanish) BRAZIL COACH, CARLO ANCELOTTI, SAYING: “It’s been a wonderful 15 days in every sense. The reception I’ve received from the Brazilian people has been wonderful. Today, at the stadium, they gave me a lot of T-shirts. The atmosphere inside the CBF, which is a fantastic environment, like a family. The attitude of the players and the good relationship that exists between them, I liked everything. All that was missing was a victory and we got it. So we’re happy." 3. CUTAWAY OF JOURNALIST ASKING QUESTION AT THE PRESS CONFERENCE HALL WHERE MEDIA ARE SEATED 4. (SOUNDBITE) (Spanish) BRAZIL COACH, CARLO ANCELOTTI, SAYING: "I think it was a good match. Of course, the ideal (thing) would be to have the same intensity as in the first half in the second, but the score favored us and we didn't risk plays like we did in the first half." 5. JOURNALISTS ATTENDING THE PRESS CONFERENCE 6. (SOUNDBITE) (Spanish) BRAZIL COACH, CARLO ANCELOTTI, SAYING: “Pressure is very important because it doesn’t allow the opponent to have time to play as they want. But there is a problem, because to press you have to run." 7. ANCELOTTI THANKING MEDIA PERSONS AND LEAVING THE PRESS CONFERENCE STORY: Brazil secured their spot at the 2026 World Cup on Tuesday (June 10), taking the second of South America's six automatic qualifying berths after champions Argentina booked their ticket in March. Brazil beat Paraguay 1-0 in Sao Paulo. A goal from Real Madrid attacker Vinicius Jr. on the stroke of halftime made it a winning home debut for Brazil's new coach Carlo Ancelotti.

Brazil extended their record as the only team to play in every edition of the World Cup, with the five-times winners set to make their 23rd appearance in next year's tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. (Production: Paulo Lopes, Renato Spyrro, Bhagya Ayyavoo)
